# Uoink

Uoink is the YouTube layer for any AI: a local-first Chrome extension and helper that turns YouTube videos into structured markdown corpora for Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and MCP-capable agents. It writes transcript, screenshots, comments, metadata, and JSON sidecars to disk; optional BYO Anthropic features add Comment Intelligence, Hook Type classification, and Entity Extraction.

Canonical URLs:
- https://uoink.video/
- https://uoink.video/creators
- https://uoink.video/research
- https://uoink.video/agents
- https://uoink.video/agent-docs
- https://uoink.video/install
- https://uoink.video/hooks
- https://uoink.video/privacy
- https://uoink.video/installed

Source: https://github.com/ryanbiddy/uoink
Contact: hi@uoink.video

Product notes:
- Local-first: Uoink does not upload a user's video corpus to Uoink servers.
- Current platform: Windows helper live; Mac v2.1 is in queue.
- Chrome Web Store listing is pending; sideload is available through GitHub Releases.
- MCP: stdio is the officially supported transport; local HTTP JSON-RPC is experimental.
- Optional AI calls: Comment Intelligence, Hook Type, and Entity Extraction use the user's own Anthropic API key and stay off by default.
- Skill: Uoink ships an Operator Skill using the agentskills.io open standard.

Page map:
/ explains the product and install state.
/creators explains creator/operator workflows.
/research explains the local corpus, topic routing, citations, and entity graph.
/agents explains MCP tools, transports, and agent integrations.
/agent-docs is the reference page for setup and tool signatures.
/install explains the helper, Chrome extension, file locations, and from-source path.
/hooks documents the nine Hook Type categories.
/privacy documents the local-first data posture.
/installed is the first-run page opened after successful helper installation.
